```{r}
library(tidyverse)
library(ggplot2)
library(quantmod)
library(data.table)
library(ggthemes)
library(tidyquant)
library(purrr)
library(alphavantager)
library(forecast)
library(eventstudyr)

api_key <- "16eGcenELfs3j9AVtmjBNa9Z5Fj8btivUyBJQSr3"


setwd("~/Documents/GitHub/ENVS_Seniorthesis")
getwd()
```


```{r}
disasters <- read_csv("data/tx_disaster_raw.csv") 


disasters_clean <- disasters %>% 
  filter(Type %in% c("Tropical Cyclone", "Winter Storm", "Severe Storm")) %>%
  mutate(
    `Begin Date` = mdy(`Begin Date`),
    `End Date` = mdy(`End Date`),
    Begin = `Begin Date`,
    End = `End Date`,
    window_start = Begin - days(7), #time series window 
    window_end   = End + days(7)
  ) %>%
  select(-`Begin Date`, -`End Date`)

cyclone <- disasters_clean %>%
  filter(Type == "Tropical Cyclone") %>%
  mutate(
    window_start = as.Date(window_start),
    window_end = as.Date(window_end),
  )


sstorm <- disasters_clean %>%
  filter(Type == "Severe Storm")

wstorm <- disasters_clean %>%
  filter(Type == "Winter Storm")

```
test
```{r}
cyclone_expanded <- cyclone %>%
  mutate(
    date_seq = map2(window_start, window_end, seq, by = "day")
  ) %>%
  unnest(date_seq) %>%
  rename(Date = date_seq) 
```


Date intervals 
```{r}
symbols <- c("CL=F", "BZ=F")

for (i in 1:nrow(cyclone)) {
  start_date <- cyclone$window_start[i]
  end_date   <- cyclone$window_end[i]
  
  cat("Fetching data for Tropical Cyclone", i, "from", start_date, "to", end_date, "\n")
  
  # --- Fetch WTI (West Texas Intermediate) ---
  wti <- tryCatch(
    getSymbols("CL=F", src = "yahoo", from = start_date, to = end_date, auto.assign = FALSE),
    error = function(e) NULL
  )
  
  # --- Fetch Brent ---
  brent <- tryCatch(
    getSymbols("BZ=F", src = "yahoo", from = start_date, to = end_date, auto.assign = FALSE),
    error = function(e) NULL
  )
  
  # --- Combine if successful ---
  if (!is.null(wti) & !is.null(brent)) {
    
    merged <- merge(wti, brent)
    names(merged) <- c(
      "WTI.Open", "WTI.High", "WTI.Low", "WTI.Close", "WTI.Volume", "WTI.Adjusted",
      "Brent.Open", "Brent.High", "Brent.Low", "Brent.Close", "Brent.Volume", "Brent.Adjusted"
    )
    
    merged <- data.frame(Date = index(merged), coredata(merged))
    merged$Event_ID <- i
    merged$Type <- cyclone$Type[i]
    
    price_data_list[[i]] <- merged
  }
  
  # small delay to prevent throttling
  Sys.sleep(2)
}

# 4’ĖÅŌāŻ Combine all event windows into one tidy dataframe
cyclone_prices <- bind_rows(price_data_list)
```

```{r}
test_df <- cyclone_expanded %>% 
  left_join(cyclone_prices, by = "Date") 
  
francine <- test_df %>% filter(Event == "Hurricane FrancineSeptember 2024") %>%
  mutate(
    Date = as.Date(Date),
    Spread = Brent.Close - WTI.Close,
    Day_Relative = as.numeric(Date - Begin[1]) # 0 = landfall
  )

ggplot(francine, aes(x = Date)) +
  geom_line(aes(y = WTI.Close, color = "WTI")) +
  geom_line(aes(y = Brent.Close, color = "Brent")) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = as.numeric(unique(francine$Begin)), linetype = "dashed", color = "red") +
  labs(
    title = "WTI vs Brent Prices Around Hurricane Francine",
    y = "Price (USD per barrel)",
    x = "Date",
    color = "Series"
  ) +
  theme_minimal()



```

```{r}

# define pre- and post-event periods (7 days before / 7 days after)
francine <- francine %>%
  mutate(
    period = case_when(
      Date < Begin[1] ~ "Pre",
      Date >= Begin[1] ~ "Post"
    )
  )

# visualize spread change
ggplot(francine, aes(x = Date, y = Spread, color = period)) +
  geom_line(size = 1) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = as.numeric(unique(francine$Begin)), linetype = "dashed", color = "red") +
  labs(
    title = "BrentŌĆōWTI Spread Before and After Hurricane Francine",
    y = "Spread (USD)",
    x = "Date"
  ) +
  theme_minimal()

# run a two-sample t-test
pre  <- francine %>% filter(period == "Pre")  %>% pull(Spread)
post <- francine %>% filter(period == "Post") %>% pull(Spread)

t_test_result <- t.test(pre, post, alternative = "two.sided")

t_test_result

```

```{r}
francine_filled <- francine %>%
  complete(Date = seq(min(Date), max(Date), by = "day")) %>%
  fill(WTI.Close, Brent.Close, .direction = "down") %>%
  mutate(
    Spread = Brent.Close - WTI.Close,
    period = if_else(Date < Begin[1], "Pre", "Post")
  )

ggplot(francine_filled, aes(x = Date, y = Spread, color = period)) +
  geom_line(size = 1.1) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = as.numeric(unique(francine_filled$Begin)), 
             linetype = "dashed", color = "red") +
  labs(
    title = "BrentŌĆōWTI Spread (Weekend-Filled) Around Hurricane Francine",
    y = "Spread (USD per barrel)",
    x = "Date",
    color = "Period"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 13)

pre  <- francine_filled %>% filter(period == "Pre")  %>% pull(Spread)
post <- francine_filled %>% filter(period == "Post") %>% pull(Spread)

pre  <- francine_filled %>% filter(period == "Pre")  %>% pull(Spread)
post <- francine_filled %>% filter(period == "Post") %>% pull(Spread)

t_test_filled <- t.test(pre, post, alternative = "two.sided")
t_test_filled
```

```{r}
library(httr)
library(jsonlite)

url <- "https://api.eia.gov/v2/electricity/retail-sales/data/"

params <- list(
  api_key = api_key,
  `data[]` = "price",
  `facets[stateid][]` = "CO",
  `facets[sectorid][]` = "RES",
  frequency = "monthly"
)

response <- GET(url, query = params)
data <- fromJSON(content(response, "text"))

# Flatten the JSON structure into a dataframe
df <- as.data.frame(data$response$data)
head(df)
```
